description: The AudioDeviceVolume component only functions in dash space. If ``AudioDeviceIndex`` is negative, then it uses the user's set default audio device. This component is used to manage the volume of an input device like a microphone for one example. This is a settings component.

fields.AudioDeviceIndex: The audio input device by OS audio manager index to modify the volume for.
fields.Volume: The volume the input device should have.
fields.NormalizedVolume: The component uses this value to boost the output noise to a higher volume when it is too quiet or reduce the volume when it is too loud.
fields.Smoothing: How fast to reduce or increase the volume when it gets too loud or quiet
fields.Power: The amount to boost the volume overall before normalization.
